continuation_reason: 100 cards audited (>= 100 minimum). Target of 25 findings not reached: fixes_implemented=4, engine_gaps_documented=2. Next run resumes at Hop's Wooloo | JTG | 135 | sv09-135.

tcgdex_effects_extracted.name: Grand Tree
tcgdex_effects_extracted.raw_text: Once during each player's turn, that player may search their deck for a Stage 1 Pokémon that evolves from 1 of their Basic Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. If that Pokémon was evolved in this way, that player may search their deck for a Stage 2 Pokémon that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, that player shuffles their deck. (Players can't evolve a Basic Pokémon during their first turn or a Basic Pokémon that was put into play this turn.)
tcgdex_effects_extracted.reason: trainer card always requires a handler

implementation_evidence.source_evidence: No sv07-136 references outside noop registration/tests; no stadium-use evolution flow found.

assertions: registry uses _noop
no active stadium evolution flow exists for sv07-136

audit_ledger.notes: Grand Tree remains a true engine gap: the card is only registered to _noop in trainers.py and there is no USE_STADIUM/search-from-deck evolution flow that can apply one or two chained evolutions with the card's first-turn and in-play timing restrictions.

tcgdex_effects_extracted.name: Granite Cave
tcgdex_effects_extracted.raw_text: Steven's Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) take 30 less damage from attacks from the opponent's Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
tcgdex_effects_extracted.reason: trainer card always requires a handler

implementation_evidence.source_evidence: Run 43 subtracts 30 damage for Steven's Pokémon in both active and bench attack-damage paths.

assertions: reduces active attack damage by 30 after W/R
does not reduce damage for non-Steven's Pokémon
does not reduce damage when Granite Cave is not in play
damage reduction is clamped at 0
reduces bench damage to Steven's Pokémon by 30

audit_ledger.notes: Granite Cave was registered to _noop only; run 43 added active and bench damage reduction paths in attacks.py and now has focused regression coverage for Steven's Active/Bench reduction, non-Steven bypass, no-stadium bypass, and zero-clamp behavior.

assertions: adds +1 retreat cost to the opposing Active
Jamming Tower suppresses the tool
active holder also pays +1 retreat cost
benched holder does not increase retreat cost

audit_ledger.notes: Gravity Gemstone was registered to _noop only; run 43 wired its +1 Retreat Cost effect for both Active Pokémon in actions.py and now covers opponent-tax, holder-self-tax, benched-holder bypass, and Jamming Tower suppression.

tcgdex_effects_extracted.name: Haban Berry
tcgdex_effects_extracted.raw_text: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is damaged by an attack from your opponent's {N} Pokémon, it takes 60 less damage (after applying Weakness and Resistance), and discard this card.
tcgdex_effects_extracted.reason: trainer card always requires a handler

implementation_evidence.source_evidence: Run 43 added Dragon matching in get_tool_damage_bonus and berry discard trigger handling in _apply_damage.

assertions: reduces Dragon attack damage by 60
discards the Berry after it triggers
non-Dragon damage is not reduced
the Berry is not discarded on non-Dragon damage
current engine applies no Haban Berry reduction/discard on bench damage
Jamming Tower prevents both reduction and discard trigger

audit_ledger.notes: Haban Berry was registered to _noop but missing from the berry damage-reduction/discard hooks; run 43 added Dragon-type matching plus Jamming Tower-safe discard handling for the active-damage path. The current engine still treats passive Tool damage-reduction as an Active-only approximation, so Dragon bench damage does not reduce or discard Haban Berry; test coverage records that limitation explicitly.

audit_ledger.notes: Heavy Baton remains a true engine gap: base.py has a partial KO trigger, but it always moves up to 3 Basic Energy to bench[0] instead of supporting the card's required “to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like” distribution choice.

implementation_evidence.effect_name: Hop's Choice Band passive tool logic
implementation_evidence.source_evidence: Run 43 moved +30 into _apply_damage pre-W/R path and gated attack-cost reduction in actions.py.

assertions: adds 30 damage before Weakness and Resistance

behavioral_evidence.effect_name: Hop's Choice Band Jamming Tower damage suppression
assertions: Jamming Tower suppresses the +30 damage bonus
non-Hop's Pokémon do not receive the +30 bonus
Hop's Pokémon without the Tool do not receive the +30 bonus
reduces attached Hop's Pokémon attack cost by {C}
Jamming Tower suppresses the tool

audit_ledger.notes: Hop's Choice Band previously applied its +30 damage after Weakness/Resistance in base.py; run 43 moved the bonus to the pre-W/R damage path and now has focused regression coverage for the intended damage timing, Jamming Tower suppression of both damage and cost effects, non-Hop bypass, and no-tool bypass.

behavioral_accounting_note: audit-quality-v4 behavioral_rows_verified counts only risky rows with result=no-issue or result=behavioral-unverified. The fixed findings for Granite Cave, Gravity Gemstone, Haban Berry, and Hop's Choice Band include focused existing-test behavioral evidence, but they are intentionally excluded from the top-level behavioral coverage totals.
